body:
## Summary

- add `--enabled-only` to `quartz plugin install` / `quartz plugin restore`
- make `npm run build` restore only plugins enabled in `quartz.config.yaml`
- skip `npm install && npm run build` for plugins that already ship pre-built `dist/`
- document that disabled plugins stay in config/lockfile but are not restored during normal builds

This keeps disabled/template opt-in plugins available without making every build clone/build them. It also matches the Quartz v5 plugin guidance that community plugins should ship committed `dist/` so installs can skip the build cycle.

## Validation

- `npm run install-plugins` on clean `.quartz`: restored 32 enabled plugins, skipped disabled plugins, completed in ~17s
- full cold `npm run build`: completed in ~22s
- `npm run check`

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to `README.md`, `package.json`, `quartz/bootstrap-cli.mjs`, and `quartz/cli/plugin-git-handlers.js`; no unrelated refactor or formatting churn. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The filter reuses existing plugin data helpers like `readPluginsJson()` and `extractPluginName()` instead of adding a parallel config parser: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/quartz/cli/plugin-git-handlers.js#L52-L72 |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds a typed boolean `--enabled-only` option for both `plugin install` and `plugin restore`, passed as `{ enabledOnly }`: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/quartz/bootstrap-cli.mjs#L127-L134 |
| coding_workflow/generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Default CLI behavior remains all plugins; only `npm run install-plugins` opts normal builds into `--enabled-only`, preserving the manual full restore/install escape hatch: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/package.json#L18-L24 |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README now states that build/check-site restore enabled plugins and disabled plugins remain in config/lockfile but are not restored during normal builds: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/README.md#L117-L121 |

## Notes

- Verification passed after installing dependencies: `npm run check`, CLI help for both new flags, and `npm run install-plugins`.
- Functional restore restored 32 enabled plugins and skipped disabled lockfile entries such as `article-title`, `bases-page`, `canvas-page`, `explorer`, `hard-line-breaks`, and `tag-list`.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #16.

No rubric changes.

I found no trusted PR conversation content that warranted learning a new durable rubric. PR #16 had one human comment from `lolipopshock` requesting `/review`, no review comments, no reviews, and one agent-authored rubric review. The agent review is advisory only and was not endorsed by a trusted contributor in the conversation. Existing rubrics already cover the implementation qualities noted there, such as surgical changes, docs sync, structured inputs, and simple reuse.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #16 speeds up Quartz plugin restore by adding `--enabled-only` to plugin install/restore and wiring normal builds to restore only plugins enabled in `quartz.config.yaml`.
- It also skips `npm install && npm run build` for plugins that already ship a prebuilt `dist/`, matching the intended Quartz v5 community plugin flow.
- Documentation was updated to explain that disabled plugins can remain in config/lockfile without being restored during normal builds.
- The PR is currently merged at `b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec`.

## Review
The reviews agree there are no blocking defects and that the PR is narrow, aligned with existing helpers, and validated through build/check flows. One reviewer raised two non-blocking warnings that are still supported by the current PR diff.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Fast path can bypass plugin failure isolation | WARNING | The prebuilt-`dist` path calls `linkPeerPlugins()` outside the `try` used by the slow build path, so a peer-linking error can abort the caller loop instead of counting one plugin failure. |
| `--enabled-only` fallback is silent | WARNING | If plugin config cannot be read, `--enabled-only` silently restores the full lockfile, making the optimization appear to work when it was ignored. |
| Enabled flag semantics differ from loader | INFO | The restore filter treats missing `enabled` as enabled, while `loadQuartzConfig` filters on truthiness; current config entries are explicit, so this is not observable today. |
| Dedicated unit coverage is absent | INFO | No targeted unit test covers enabled-only filtering or prebuilt-`dist` skipping, but reviewers noted broad manual/integration validation and low residual risk. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion had no inline review comments or review threads before synthesis.
- Posted two new inline comments: [`linkPeerPlugins` fast path](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/16#discussion_r3359238244) and [`--enabled-only` silent fallback](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/16#discussion_r3359238870).
- Rubrics review passed, including surgical scope, reuse of existing helpers, explicit CLI option wiring, and README sync.
- Reviewer validation included `npm run check`, `npm run install-plugins`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `git diff --check`.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Fast path can bypass plugin failure isolation</summary>

**Cause:** [`buildPlugin`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/quartz/cli/plugin-git-handlers.js#L21-L44) returns early when `dist/` exists, but calls `linkPeerPlugins()` before the caught slow path.

**Candidate solutions:** Wrap the fast path in the same `try/catch`, or refactor peer linking into one common caught block used by both build and skip paths.

**Comments:** This is non-blocking, but it is a real regression in per-plugin failure isolation.
</details>

<details>
<summary>`--enabled-only` fallback is silent</summary>

**Cause:** [`getLockfilePluginEntries`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/quartz/cli/plugin-git-handlers.js#L63-L73) returns all lockfile entries when `getEnabledPluginNames()` returns `null`.

**Candidate solutions:** Log a warning when the config cannot be read, or throw if `enabledOnly` was explicitly requested and no enabled set is available.

**Comments:** Falling back to the old full-restore behavior is safe, but currently invisible.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Enabled flag semantics differ from loader</summary>

**Cause:** The restore filter uses `entry.enabled !== false`, while [`loadQuartzConfig`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/b9fd3ff5228ad82cd28ebe6538987a0a59ddb3ec/quartz/plugins/loader/config-loader.ts#L240) uses truthiness.

**Candidate solutions:** Align restore filtering to `entry.enabled === true` if the loader’s behavior is the intended source of truth.

**Comments:** Current config sets `enabled` explicitly on every entry, so this is future-proofing rather than a present defect.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Dedicated unit coverage is absent</summary>

**Cause:** The new CLI behavior is covered by manual/integration validation, not focused tests around the filtering helper or prebuilt-`dist` path.

**Candidate solutions:** Add narrow tests for enabled-only lockfile filtering and build skipping if this CLI area gets a test harness.

**Comments:** Reviewers did not treat this as blocking because the repo has limited existing CLI test coverage and the PR was validated end to end.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
HUMAN_DECISION: Remaining concerns are non-blocking follow-up choices on an already merged PR, so maintainers should decide whether to open a follow-up rather than trigger automated PR changes.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Speed up Quartz plugin restore
